source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Main St., Sitka, Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard showing Main Street in Sitka, Alaska, with St. Michael's Cathedral bell tower visible in the background and a Tlingit clan house with painted totemic design on the right. The image captures the unique cultural intersection of Russian colonial architecture and Tlingit Native heritage. Card number 473, produced by Winter & Pond studio, Juneau, between 1900 and 1940.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of Main Street, Sitka, Alaska showing St. Michael's Cathedral bell tower and a Tlingit clan house with totemic design
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
